findings.description: Superlative impact claim without citation: 'The most impactful action: contact your senators.' Stated as empirical fact rather than advocacy recommendation.
findings.suggestion: Hedge to 'Among the most impactful actions' or cite constituent correspondence effectiveness research (e.g., Congressional Management Foundation surveys on constituent influence).
findings.context: The most impactful action: contact your senators. The ICESCR stalled because no organized constituency demanded ratification.

findings.description: Single-cause attribution for ICESCR stalling: 'stalled because no organized constituency demanded ratification.' The 1979 hearings and subsequent inaction involved multiple factors (Cold War politics, ABA justiciability concerns, competing legislative priorities). Single-cause framing oversimplifies.
findings.suggestion: Qualify with 'in part because' or 'a key factor: no organized constituency demanded ratification.' Alternatively, add a parenthetical acknowledging multiple factors.
findings.context: The ICESCR stalled because no organized constituency demanded ratification. Each constituent letter shifts the political calculation.

findings.description: Comparative effectiveness claim without citation: 'Send by mail (most impact).' Congressional Management Foundation research supports mail over email for constituent influence, but no citation provided.
findings.suggestion: Add a brief parenthetical source or link to CMF research on constituent communication effectiveness, or soften to 'traditionally carries more weight.'
findings.context: Send by mail (most impact), email, or phone call to (202) 224-3121

findings.description: Effectiveness claim for structured dialogue attributed to Braver Angels but without specific evidence: 'structured dialogue across political differences produces common ground more effectively than persuasion.' The Braver Angels link provides organizational context but not the effectiveness evidence.
findings.suggestion: Either cite a specific Braver Angels impact study or soften to 'aims to produce common ground' rather than asserting comparative effectiveness.
findings.context: Braver Angels...demonstrates that structured dialogue across political differences produces common ground more effectively than persuasion.

epistemic_flags: All 4 findings cluster in fair-witness dimension on a single advocacy page — this may reflect the inherent tension between advocacy register and fair witness standards rather than quality defects
CMF research citation suggested for f1/f3 based on agent knowledge — verify the specific source exists before acting on the suggestion
